2234	        self,
2235	        username: str = "",
2236	        password: str = "",
2237	        database: str = "",
2238	        charset: Optional[int] = None,
2239	        password1: str = "",
2240	        password2: str = "",
2241	        password3: str = "",
2242	        oci_config_file: str = "",
2243	        oci_config_profile: str = "",
2244	        openid_token_file: str = "",
2245	    ) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
2246	        """Changes the current logged in user.
2247	
2248	        It also causes the specified database to become the default (current)
2249	        database. It is also possible to change the character set using the
2250	        charset argument. The character set passed during initial connection
2251	        is reused if no value of charset is passed via this method.
2252	
2253	        Args:
2254	            username: New account's username.
2255	            password: New account's password.
2256	            database: Database to become the default (current) database.
2257	            charset: Client charset (see [1]), only the lower 8-bits.
2258	            password1: New account's password factor 1 - it's used instead
2259	                       of `password` if set (higher precedence).
2260	            password2: New account's password factor 2.
2261	            password3: New account's password factor 3.
2262	            oci_config_file: OCI configuration file location (path-like string).
2263	            oci_config_profile: OCI configuration profile location (path-like string).
2264	            openid_token_file: OpenID Connect token file location (path-like string).
2265	
2266	        Returns:
2267	            ok_packet: Dictionary containing the OK packet information.
2268	
2269	        Examples:
2270	            ```
2271	            >>> cnx.cmd_change_user(username='', password='', database='', charset=33)
2272	            ```
2273	
2274	        References:
2275	            [1]: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/dev/mysql-server/latest/\
2276	                page_protocol_basic_character_set.html#a_protocol_character_set
2277	        """

1191	        # otherwise, we run a verification and update the charset ID.

1194	                raise ValueError("charset must be an integer")
1195	            if charset < 0:
1196	                raise ValueError("charset should be either zero or a postive integer")

2232	    @abstractmethod
2233	    def cmd_change_user(
2234	        self,
2235	        username: str = "",
2236	        password: str = "",
2237	        database: str = "",
2238	        charset: Optional[int] = None,
2239	        password1: str = "",
2240	        password2: str = "",
2241	        password3: str = "",
2242	        oci_config_file: str = "",
2243	        oci_config_profile: str = "",
2244	        openid_token_file: str = "",
2245	    ) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
2246	        """Changes the current logged in user.
2247	
2248	        It also causes the specified database to become the default (current)
2249	        database. It is also possible to change the character set using the
2250	        charset argument. The character set passed during initial connection
2251	        is reused if no value of charset is passed via this method.
2252	
2253	        Args:
2254	            username: New account's username.
2255	            password: New account's password.
2256	            database: Database to become the default (current) database.
2257	            charset: Client charset (see [1]), only the lower 8-bits.
2258	            password1: New account's password factor 1 - it's used instead
2259	                       of `password` if set (higher precedence).
2260	            password2: New account's password factor 2.
2261	            password3: New account's password factor 3.
2262	            oci_config_file: OCI configuration file location (path-like string).
2263	            oci_config_profile: OCI configuration profile location (path-like string).
2264	            openid_token_file: OpenID Connect token file location (path-like string).
2265	
2266	        Returns:
2267	            ok_packet: Dictionary containing the OK packet information.
2268	
2269	        Examples:
2270	            ```
2271	            >>> cnx.cmd_change_user(username='', password='', database='', charset=33)
2272	            ```
2273	
2274	        References:
2275	            [1]: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/dev/mysql-server/latest/\
2276	                page_protocol_basic_character_set.html#a_protocol_character_set
2277	        """
2278
